name: Cy Coleman
description: American composer, songwriter, and jazz pianist (1929-2004)
abstract: Cy Coleman (born Seymour Kaufman; June 14, 1929 – November 18, 2004) was an American composer, songwriter, and jazz pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: It would be my trio and maybe Cy Coleman. Then at another point there was a trio ... They were working opposite us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: We did a Cy Coleman date because our bass player at the time, Bill Takas was Cy’s bass player and he contracted.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ... Cy Coleman’s “My City.” [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, it sounded darn good.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: There’s a great record of Peggy Lee singing Cy Coleman’s “I’ve Got Your Number.”
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015
